title: Gonocarpus cordiger
taxonomy: Gonocarpus cordiger is a species of plant in the family Haloragaceae. It belongs to the order Haloragales and the class Magnoliopsida. Gonocarpus cordiger is further classified as a member of the subclass Magnoliidae and the division Magnoliophyta. This classification helps to group plants with similar characteristics and evolutionary relationships. The detailed classification of Gonocarpus cordiger provides valuable information for botanists and researchers studying plant diversity.
uses: Gonocarpus cordiger is known for its traditional use in Australian Aboriginal medicine. It has been used to treat various ailments, including fever and rheumatic conditions. The plant's leaves and stems are often used in infusions to create medicinal teas. Some studies suggest that Gonocarpus cordiger may exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, although more research is needed to confirm these findings. Further investigation is required to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal applications.

intro: Gonocarpus cordiger, also known as Cordia, is a plant species in the family Boraginaceae, characterized by its unique, pinnate leaves and small, white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Gonocarpus cordiger include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Gonocarpus cordiger is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are also used in the production of capsules, tablets, and tinctures.
